license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.pyfdptn
title: ChroSpin - ProteinG
author: David Frommholz

steps_raw.instruction: Add up to 500 µl sample to the top of the column and incubate the column with end-over-end mixing or occasional inversion for 3 minutes. 00:03:00 For fast binding antibodies the incubation time can be decreased to 30 seconds. For slow binding antibodies the incubation time can be increased to 10 minutes. Longer incubation time is possible, however, this will also increase background. For optimal binding and purity, the pH of the sample should be 7 - 8 and should contain 150 - 300 mM NaCl. An easy way to achieve this is by adding 1/11 volume 10x PBS to your sample. Purification works best with an antibody concentration of 2 - 3 mg in your sample.
Centrifuge the column for 30 - 60 seconds at 1000 g and place the column into a clean receiver tube. It is advisable to keep the flow-through and wash fractions for later analysis, e.g. SDS-PAGE.

Add 500 µl wash buffer to the column, close the lid, and invert 5 times. Centrifuge the column for 30 - 60 seconds at 1000 g and empty the receiver tube. In case of unspecific hydrophobic and/or ionic interactions include up to 1 % Tween-20 and/or up to 0.5 M NaCl in the wash buffer.
Place the column into a clean receiver tube, add 200 µl elution buffer to the column, close the lid, and invert three times. Centrifuge the column for 30 - 60 seconds at 1000 g.
Leave receiver tube in place. Add 200 µl elution buffer to the column, close the lid and centrifuge for 30 - 60 seconds at 1000 g. Repeat once more.

Remove the bottom cap, add 500 µl wash buffer to the column, close the lid, invert sharply 3 - 5 times, and centrifuge 30 - 60 seconds at 1000 g. If you work with a used column, remove the storage solution by centrifugation first.
Repeat the previous step. go to step #5 For increased purity, repeat the washing step up to 5 times.

Wash the column by adding 500 µl elution buffer and centrifuge at 1000 g for 30 - 60 seconds. Repeat this step with 500 µl wash buffer and 500 µl deionized water. Then, close the bottom of the column, add 500 µl 20 % ethanol or wash buffer (contains 0.05 % (w/v) sodium azide). Close the lid and store at room temperature or at 4 - 8 °C. Alternative for long-term storage: Dry the open (top and bottom) column in an oven at 80 °C for at least 2 hours or over night. Make sure the bottom stopper is completely dry, too. Put on the column's outlet, close the lid and store the column closed at room temperature.
Add two drops of neutralization solution to the eluate and mix gently.
Do you want to purify antibodies or sanitize your column? Please choose below.

After purification: wash the column with 500 µl elution buffer, centrifuge the column for 30 - 60 seconds at 1000 g, and repeat with 500 µl wash buffer. If the column was stored before: remove the bottom cap and centrifuge the column for 30 - 60 seconds at 1000 g. Then, add 500 µl of wash buffer and centrifuge the column for 30 - 60 seconds at 1000 g.
Close the bottom of the column, add 500 µl sanitization solution. Close the lid and incubate for one hour at RT.
Remove the bottom cap and centrifuge the column for 30 - 60 seconds at 1000 g. Wash three times with 500 µl of deionized water.

Close the bottom of the column, add 500 µl wash buffer (contains 0.05 % (w/v) sodium azide). Close the lid and store at room temperature or at 4 - 8 °C. It is recommended to wait at least 12 hours before the next purification. Alternative for long-term storage: Dry the open (top and bottom) column in an oven at 80 °C for at least 2 hours or over night. Make sure the bottom stopper is completely dry, too. Put on the column's outlet, close the lid and store the column closed at room temperature.
